item.title: The hardware needs of our mail system (as of mid 2026) 摘要
item.description:
A university department's email system requires at least seven dedicated servers: an external mail gateway, an anti-spam/anti-virus server, a central mail machine, a spam forwarding server, an unauthenticated mail submission server, an IMAP/authenticated SMTP server with local storage, and a webmail frontend. These servers rely on shared infrastructure like NFS fileservers and DNS. The IMAP server is the most powerful, with 64 GB RAM and SSDs, and costs are significant, especially for new hardware. The system serves over 1,000 active users with about 1.5 TB of inboxes and 10,000 inbound messages daily.
标签: email, system administration, university IT, server infrastructure, IMAP, anti-spam, NFS, hardware costs, mail server, IT operations

item.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash: more expensive, but Google plan to use it for everything 摘要
item.description:
Google released Gemini 3.5 Flash at Google I/O, skipping preview and going straight to general availability. It powers Gemini app, AI Mode in Search, Antigravity platform, and enterprise products. The model supports 1M input tokens and 65K output tokens, with a knowledge cutoff of January 2025. A new Interactions API (beta) offers server-side history management. Pricing increased significantly: 3x the cost of 3 Flash Preview and 6x of 3.1 Flash-Lite, at $1.50/M input and $9/M output. This follows industry trends of rising LLM prices. Gemini 3.5 Pro is expected next month.
标签: Google, AI, Gemini, LLM, pricing, Google I/O, API, generative AI, model release
